subject: Social Science (History)
chapter_name: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the political and economic factors that led to the rise of nationalism in India
Evaluate the role of the Indian National Congress in shaping the freedom movement
Compare the approaches of moderate and extremist leaders in the early nationalist phase
Examine the role of mass participation in transforming the national movement
Understand the strategies used in major movements such as Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience, and Quit India
Analyze how print media, political debates, and public meetings helped spread nationalist ideas
Evaluate the historical developments that led to India's independence in 1947

lesson_plan.title: Origins of Indian Nationalism
topics_covered: Political awareness in the late 19th century
student_activities: Analyze causes for the rise of nationalism
Discuss the economic impact of British policies on India

lesson_plan.title: Formation and Early Role of the Indian National Congress
topics_covered: Establishment of the Indian National Congress in 1885
student_activities: Create a timeline of early Congress activities
Discuss the significance of the Congress in early nationalism

lesson_plan.title: Moderate and Extremist Phases of the National Movement
student_activities: Prepare a comparison table of moderate and extremist leaders
Explain the contributions of major leaders

lesson_plan.title: Spread of Nationalism and Role of the Masses
topics_covered: Participation of peasants, workers, and students
Role of women in the national movement
Expansion of nationalist ideas across regions
student_activities: Identify different social groups involved in the movement
Discuss how participation strengthened the movement

lesson_plan.title: Major National Movements
student_activities: Prepare a timeline of major movements
Analyze the significance of each movement

lesson_plan.title: Political Developments Leading to Independence
student_activities: Identify key events leading to independence
Prepare a summary of major political developments

lesson_plan.title: Impact of the National Movement and Revision

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the causes and strategies of the national movement rather than only memorizing events. Use timelines and comparative discussions to help students understand how different phases of the movement contributed to India’s independence.
